nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) Yellow and green elephants are nice.
(2) John is an elephant.
(3) John is yellow and green.
(4) John is nice?
Proof steps:
(1) If some X is yellow and X is green and X is an elephant then normally X is a good elephant  [sentence 1]
(2) John is yellow  [sentence 3]
(3) If John is an elephant and John is green then normally John is a good elephant  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If John is a good elephant then answer holds  [sentence 4]
(5) assume for contradiction: the answer is no  [from question]
(6) John is not a good elephant  [from steps 4, 5]
(7) If John is an elephant then John is not green, except when John is not a good elephant  [from steps 3, 6]
(8) John is an elephant  [sentence 2]
(9) John is not green, except when John is not a good elephant  [from steps 7, 8]
(10) John is green  [sentence 3]
(11) Outstanding exception: John is not a good elephant  [from steps 9, 10]
Exceptions checked and not holding:
John is not a good elephant
